title: Explain Spoken Queries
short_answer: spoken_queries is the block that answers spoken questions.
text: In this app, spoken_queries reads routed response events, chooses the correct runner, writes a result, and provides speech_text for speech output.

title: Explain Spoken Query Router
short_answer: Spoken Query Router decides which runner answers a spoken question.
text: The router reads requests from response_queue.jsonl and routes them to HELP, memory, simple question, LLM direct, command result, or refusal.

title: Explain Help Matcher
short_answer: Help Matcher detects when a spoken phrase should go to HELP.
text: In this app, HELP phrases always go to HELP. EXPLAIN phrases go to HELP when help explain capture is enabled in system runtime state.

title: Explain Help Runner
short_answer: Help Runner searches HELP JSON files and returns the best answer.
text: The help runner scans app/system_support/HELP recursively, scores matching items, and returns answer_text and speech_text from the selected help entry.

title: Explain Memory Matcher
short_answer: Memory Matcher detects when a spoken question should use memory.
text: Memory matching should be separate from HELP. It should detect use_memory flags, memory phrases, or persona lookups, then route to the memory runner.

title: Explain Simple Question
short_answer: Simple Question is a small direct answer that does not need LLM or memory.
text: Examples are current time, current date, presence check, identity, or current mode. These answers are handled by simple question matcher and runner.

title: Explain Request Text
short_answer: request_text extracts the user text from a spoken query payload.
text: This small helper reads text, cleaned_text, or transcript_text. It keeps all spoken query runners using the same request text source.

title: Explain Speech Text
short_answer: speech_text is the version of an answer meant to be spoken.
text: spoken_queries returns both answer_text and speech_text. answer_text can be full, while speech_text should be clean and usable by the voice output layer.

title: Explain Answer Text
short_answer: answer_text is the main written answer returned by a runner.
text: answer_text is stored in spoken_query_results.jsonl and can also be written to chat history. It may be longer than speech_text.

title: Explain Refusal Runner
short_answer: Refusal Runner gives a safe fallback answer when no route matches.
text: If spoken_queries does not match HELP, memory, simple question, or LLM, the refusal runner returns a simple response instead of crashing or staying silent.
